methodology.purpose: Compare control flow, persistence shape, cancellation, retry state, tool aggregation and timeout semantics without model quality or network variance.

compatibility.v7: first-class totalMs, stepMs and chunkMs configuration

toolCalling.conclusion: AI SDK 7 top-level tool fields represent the full run. Use finalStep when code needs final-step-only semantics.

persistence.conclusion: Persist a version-neutral event model and keep raw SDK response messages as audit evidence instead of treating their count or shape as a stable database contract.

failure.conclusion: Both fixtures expose a tool-error part, but full-run aggregation differs. A failed call is not a successful tool result.

abort.invariant: Persist completed tool state before final generation, mark the run aborted, and enforce idempotency so a resumed request cannot execute the same tool twice.

retry.conclusion: Tool retries are an application policy. Track each attempt separately from the SDK step and expose one final tool result to the model.

timeout.conclusion: AI SDK 7 provides first-class total, step and chunk timeout configuration. AI SDK 6 requires an application-owned AbortController and timer for equivalent control.

conditions: Audit top-level toolCalls and toolResults reads.
Normalize persistence before switching production traffic.
Propagate AbortSignal through providers and tools.
Define idempotency keys and explicit retry records for side-effecting tools.
Run the same deterministic fixture in CI before upgrading provider packages.
